what_it_does: Defines a systemd service that launches a Python session supervisor from a virtual environment, passes it port 8771, keeps it running under company.target, and sends stdout/stderr to the journal.
observations: Description states it owns a supervised Claude Code fleet and exposes HTTP on 127.0.0.1:8771
StandardOutput/StandardError go to journal with SyslogIdentifier company-session-supervisor
standouts: Explicitly described as owning a supervised Claude Code fleet
Port 8771 is passed as a positional argument to the script
concerns: Hardcoded /home/tim/company path makes the unit user- and host-specific
No sandboxing directives (User, Group, ProtectSystem, etc.)
The 127.0.0.1:8771 binding is only in the description, not in ExecStart
Restart=on-failure could hide repeated crash loops
notes: This is the systemd service wrapper for the company session supervisor, pinning it to company.target and restarting it on failure.
questions: Is this intended as a user service or a system service?
Does runtime/session_supervisor.py actually bind to 127.0.0.1:8771 or only to port 8771?
What does 'supervised Claude Code fleet' mean in this context?
Why is /home/tim hardcoded rather than parameterized?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches what it does: it starts and supervises the session supervisor process; the HTTP binding on 127.0.0.1:8771 is described but only the port number appears in ExecStart.
intention_signals: Provide a durable, auto-restarting local supervisor for a fleet of Claude Code sessions, integrated into a company-wide systemd target.
contribution: Defines the runtime launch contract for the session supervisor: how it is started, which working directory and Python environment it uses, how it is restarted, how its logs are identified, and which systemd target groups it.
summary_for_embedding: A systemd service unit that launches /home/tim/company/.venv/bin/python runtime/session_supervisor.py 8771 from /home/tim/company, restarts on failure, logs to the journal, and belongs to company.target.
intention_for_embedding: To keep the company session supervisor running as a managed local HTTP service on port 8771 under a unified systemd target.
